mcqs.question: What is the solution to the recurrence T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n using the Master Theorem?
mcqs.explanation: For T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n, a=2, b=2, f(n)=n. n^(log_b a)=n^1=n. Since f(n)=Θ(n), this matches Case 2 of the Master Theorem, giving T(n)=Θ(n log n).

mcqs.question: Which case of the Master Theorem applies to T(n) = 4T(n/2) + n²?
mcqs.explanation: For T(n)=4T(n/2)+n², a=4, b=2, f(n)=n². n^(log_b a)=n^(log_2 4)=n². Since f(n)=Θ(n²), this matches Case 2 of the Master Theorem.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of T(n) = T(n-1) + n?
mcqs.explanation: This recurrence doesn't fit the Master Theorem form. Using substitution: T(n)=T(n-1)+n=T(n-2)+(n-1)+n=...=1+2+3+...+n=Θ(n²).

mcqs.question: For the recurrence T(n)=2T(n/4)+n^(0.5), which Master Theorem case applies?

options.text: Cannot be solved by Master Theorem

mcqs.explanation: a=2, b=4, f(n)=n^(0.5). n^(log_b a)=n^(log_4 2)=n^(0.5). Since f(n)=Θ(n^(0.5)), this is Case 2 where k=0, so T(n)=Θ(n^(0.5) log n).

mcqs.question: Which condition must be verified for Case 3 of the Master Theorem?

options.text: f(n) = O(n^(log_b a - ε)) for some ε > 0
f(n) = Ω(n^(log_b a + ε)) for some ε > 0 and af(n/b) ≤ cf(n) for some c < 1

mcqs.explanation: Case 3 requires both that f(n) grows faster than n^(log_b a) by a polynomial factor (ε > 0) and that the regularity condition af(n/b) ≤ cf(n) holds for some c < 1.
